---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-08-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

```{r}
# load packages and set working directory and source

library(psych)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sjmisc)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)
library(data.table)
library(checkmate)
library(knitr)
library(rmarkdown)
library(bookdown)
library(tinytex)

setwd("~/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Documents/GitHub/asa-abm-v2")

source("simulation/engine.R")
```

```{r}
# test run

test_results <- run_asa_simulation(n_steps = 10, initial_size = 10)
print(test_results$metrics)
```

```{r}
# check branch working in
system("git branch --show-current", intern = TRUE)
  #"main"

# check if core folder is already there
dir.exists("core")
  #TRUE
```

```{r}
# task 1.1 - create the departments (core/department.R)

# create function to assign agents (employees) to departments
assign_departments <- function(agent_dt, num_departments = 5) {
  if (!"id" %in% names(agent_dt)) {
    stop("agent_dt must have a column 'id'")
  }

# check if data.table 
setDT(agent_dt) 
  
# assign to departments 
agent_dt[, department := sample(
  paste0("Dept", 1:num_departments),
  .N,
   replace = TRUE
)]

  return(agent_dt)
}
```


```{r}
# check if it works

# create data set of agents
agents <- data.table(id = 1:100)

# assign to departments
agents <- assign_departments(agents)

#preview data set
head(agents)

#issue - not showing anything?

print(agents)

#### issue - not showing anything 


source("core/department.R")

exists("assign_departments")

### issue - not showing anything
```
